subject: Human Resources
title: Federal vs. State Employment Law
description: A micro-lesson that makes Federal vs. State Employment Law usable.

hook.text: Federal law says no paid family leave. California offers 8 weeks. New York offers 12. Which one applies? All of them.

buildup.text: Employment law operates at federal, state, and local levels—sometimes overlapping, sometimes conflicting.

discovery.text: The general rule: when laws conflict, the one most protective of employee applies. Employers must follow the strictest standard.

twist.text: Multi-state employers face a compliance maze—different rules for overtime, leave, harassment training, and termination per state.

climax.text: Remote work has made this harder. An employee in Texas working for a California company—whose law applies? Often both.

punchline.text: One country, 50 sets of rules. Compliance means knowing all of them.

quiz.question: When federal and state employment laws conflict, which applies?
options: The one most protective of the employee
